hook: You already make predictions every day — whether it will rain, if your team will win, or how likely you are to get a text back. But how do you turn those gut feelings into precise, reliable numbers? This unit gives you the mathematical tools to measure uncertainty and make decisions with confidence.
concept: Every topic in this unit builds toward one idea: assigning numbers to uncertainty in a consistent, logical way — formally captured by the concept of a *probability measure* on a sample space.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to precisely describe every possible outcome of a random process by constructing a sample space $S$ and identifying subsets called events.
Master the foundational rules that every valid probability must satisfy, including $0 \le P(A) \le 1$ and $P(S) = 1$, so your calculations are always logically consistent.
Apply the classical probability formula $P(A) = \frac{\text{number of favorable outcomes}}{\text{total outcomes}}$ to calculate exact probabilities when all outcomes are equally likely.
You will learn to combine and relate events using complement ($A^c$), union ($A \cup B$), and intersection ($A \cap B$) to answer more complex probability questions.
Use Venn diagrams as a visual tool to organize overlapping events, verify probability rules, and solve problems involving multiple events at a glance.
